Home / Services / Rodent Exclusion

Rodent Exclusion

Complete solutions to keep rats and mice out of Tampa Bay homes year-round.

Rodents seek refuge in Tampa homes during both rainy summers and cooler winter months, often entering through gaps in older home foundations or utility penetrations in newer builds. Our exclusion process starts with identifying all potential entry points - from broken roof tiles on historic homes to garage door seals in suburban developments.

We then implement physical barriers using chew-proof materials, paying special attention to vulnerable areas like attic vents and crawlspace accesses. For active infestations, we use tamper-resistant bait stations placed strategically away from children and pets, rather than risky poisons.

Post-storm inspections are especially important, as hurricanes and heavy rains can create new entry points. We also advise on landscaping changes to deter rodents in Tampa's lush environments.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Interior and exterior inspection for droppings, rub marks, and entry points
  2. 02 Step 2: Sealing all gaps with rodent-proof materials matched to your home's architecture
  3. 03 Step 3: Safe removal of existing rodents using humane traps and targeted baits
  4. 04 Step 4: Follow-up to ensure the property remains rodent-free

Benefits

  • Prevents disease transmission from rodents
  • Protects wiring and insulation from damage
  • Long-term solutions rather than temporary fixes
  • Reduces risk of snakes that follow rodent prey

Frequently Asked Questions

Why do I suddenly have rodents in my Tampa home?

Rodent activity often increases after heavy rains or nearby construction disturbs their habitats. Our waterfront and historic districts are particularly susceptible as older homes naturally develop more entry points over time.

How do you seal homes without reducing ventilation?

We use specialized mesh and vent covers that block rodents while maintaining airflow - crucial for preventing moisture buildup in Florida's humid climate.

Get a Free Estimate

Ready to get started with rodent exclusion? Contact us today.
